LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN PIERCE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT BEEN PREJUDICED BY THE FAILURE TO RECEIVE A COPY OF THE MOTION BECAUSE DEFENDANT OBTAINED A COPY OF THE MOTION AND RESPONDED ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE PROPOSED CAUSES OF ACTION ARE FATALLY DEFECTIVE. IN ADDITION, THE BETTER PRACTICE IS TO ALLOW AMENDMENT AND DEFENDANT CAN ATTACK THE PLEADING BY DEMURRER OR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. SEE CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GEN. INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 280-281 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN KRANSCO V. AMERICAN EMPIRE SURPLUS LINES INS. CO., (2000) 23 CAL.4TH 390, 407 FN. 11). THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT FACIALLY SHOW THAT THE CONVERSION CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME BARRED AND DEFENDANT RAISES A FACTUAL ISSUE. THE PROPOSED DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT DEFECTIVE. CCP 1060 [DECLARATORY RELIEF] IS NOT LIMITED TO WRITTEN INSTRUMENTS AND THE EXPRESS LANGUAGE OF THE STATUTE ALLOWS THE COURT TO MAKE A DECLARATION REGARDING THE RIGHTS OF THE PARTIES OVER PROPERTY. THERE IS LITTLE PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT FROM THE AMENDMENT GIVEN THE TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Geoffrey Spellberg of Spellberg Law Offices telephone number 415-678-3800 for Plaintiff.
Lisa Ungerer of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Reynolds, Shuey & Mintz telephone number 510-465-2233 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN PIERCE'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant has not been prejudiced by the failure to receive a copy of the motion because defendant obtained a copy of the motion and responded on the merits. Defendant fails to show that the proposed causes of action are fatally defective. In addition, the better practice is to allow amendment and defendant can attack the pleading by demurrer or summary judgment. See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court, (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280-281 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co., (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390, 407 fn. 11). The proposed amended complaint does not facially show that the conversion cause of action is time barred and defendant raises a factual issue. The proposed declaratory relief cause of action is not defective. CCP 1060 [declaratory relief] is not limited to written instruments and the express language of the statute allows the court to make a declaration regarding the rights of the parties over property. There is little prejudice to defendant from the amendment given the trial date. Order signed in open Court.
